news.headline: Michael Saylor proposes US Bitcoin reserve could generate $106 trillion in 20 years, outlines bold strategy.
news.summary: During a keynote at "Bitcoin for America" on March 11, Michael Saylor, the executive chairman and co-founder of Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), proposed a bold plan for the U.S. to establish a strategic Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) reserve to enhance American economic dominance. Saylor projected the potential value of this reserve to be between $3 trillion to $106 trillion over the next two decades. This aligns with senator Cynthia Lummis' (R-Wyo.) Bitcoin Act of 2025, which suggests acquiring 1 million BTC in a five-year period. For further details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Slotozilla study reveals rising deepfake fraud threat in cybersecurity, highlighting advanced technology usage.

The study examines the rapid advancement of this technology and its surging utilization in cybersecurity. The detailed analysis sheds light on the alarming increase in deepfake-related fraud instances and the potential risks they pose to individuals and organizations. This research underscores the importance of understanding and addressing the emerging challenges associated with deepfakes to ensure enhanced cybersecurity measures in the digital realm.

news.headline: February inflation eases, CPI falls to 2.8% (from 3.0% in January), hints at Fed rate cuts. Core CPI down to 3.1% from 3.3%.
news.summary: February saw a reduction in inflation rates, surprising economists and supporting the Federal Reserve's consideration of interest rate reductions. The Consumer Price Index headline figure dropped from 3.0% in January to 2.8% in February, falling short of the anticipated 2.9%. This decrease follows four consecutive months of inflation upticks. Monthly, CPI rose by 0.2% in February, significantly lower than January's 0.5% and below the 0.3% forecast. Core inflation, which excludes food and energy, slowed to 3.1% annually, down from January's 3.3% and below the expected 3.2%. The monthly core CPI increase was 0.2%, a drop from January's 0.4% and beneath the 0.3% projection. The shelter index notably increased by 0.3% in February.

news.headline: February CPI shows core inflation cools more than expected amid Trump travel slump, S&P 500 futures jump.
news.summary: In February, the consumer price index indicated a larger-than-anticipated decrease in core inflation, signaling consumer hesitance. Concurrently, S&P 500 futures saw a significant increase in value. The decline in CPI inflation could be attributed to a decrease in travel related to the Trump administration. This news was reported live without mentioning the source.

Top forecasters anticipate Adobe's Q1 earnings with $4.97 EPS and $5.66B revenue projections. Stock closed at $433.66.
news.summary: Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ:ADBE) is set to announce its first-quarter financial results after the market closes on March 12. Analysts are predicting earnings of $4.97 per share, up from $4.48 per share compared to the previous year. The company is expected to report quarterly revenue of $5.66 billion, surpassing the $5.18 billion recorded a year ago. Adobe recently reported fourth-quarter revenue of $5.61 billion on December 11, 2024, beating the estimated $5.54 billion. On Tuesday, Adobe shares closed at $433.66, experiencing a 0.3% decline. For more detailed analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga, where they can filter information by stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, or rating change.

news.headline: Enlighted introduces Enlighted Eazy - smart lighting system for SMBs with laser-based commissioning, 30% faster deployment.
news.summary: FREMONT, California - Siemens-owned property technology company, Enlighted, introduced Enlighted Eazy, a new smart lighting control solution tailored for small and medium-sized businesses (SMB) market. The product provides quick, user-friendly app-based setup at an affordable cost. As the sole lighting control system with laser-based commissioning available, Enlighted Eazy speeds up deployment by up to 30% compared to conventional Bluetooth methods. This innovative solution aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in the SMB sector.

news.headline: March is LFS Awareness Month, jscreen highlights importance of genetic testing with 7-time cancer survivor Lainie Jones.
news.summary: In March 12, 2025, in Atlanta, jscreen®, a national nonprofit public health initiative, highlighted the significance of genetic testing during Li-Fraumeni Syndrome (LFS) Awareness Month. This initiative was championed by Seven-Time Cancer Survivor Lainie Jones, emphasizing the life-saving impact of genetic testing. LFS is a hereditary cancer predisposition syndrome and raising awareness about it is crucial. Jscreen is actively promoting the importance of genetic testing, especially considering that individuals with LFS have a 90% risk of developing cancer within their lifetime. This initiative aims to educate the public about the benefits of genetic testing in potentially detecting and preventing cancer earlier.

news.summary: In Sacramento, California, on March 12, 2025, Straine Dental Management (SDM), a leading dentist-owned and dentist-led dental services organization (DSO), has achieved a noteworthy milestone by acquiring five exceptional dental practices in Texas.

news.headline: OsteoStrong hosts webinar on GLP-1's impact on bone health and osteoporosis research. CEO and engineer to discuss findings.
news.summary: On March 12, 2025, in Nashville, Tennessee, OsteoStrong, a prominent figure in osteogenic loading technology, will host a live webinar. The event will showcase CEO Kyle Zagrodzky and biomedical engineer Dr. John Jaquish. The focus will be on recently published research regarding osteoporosis and its side effects. This webinar seeks to shed light on crucial findings in the field.

news.headline: AGM schedule confirmed, to be held on June 15th with a total of 500 attendees. Key topics to be discussed.
news.summary: A recent report revealed that the global economy is projected to grow by 4.5% in 2021, following a 3.5% contraction in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Advanced economies are expected to grow by 5.4% this year, while emerging market and developing economies are forecasted to grow by 6%. Inflation rates are predicted to rise to an average of 3.6% in advanced economies and 5.7% in emerging markets. The report also highlighted that global trade volumes are estimated to increase by 8.4% in 2021. Additionally, total global debt surged to a record high of $289 trillion in the first quarter of 2021, representing 355% of global GDP. Despite the optimistic growth outlook, uncertainties remain due to evolving pandemic situations and varying vaccination rates across countries.

news.headline: Asia-Pacific digital soil mapping market valued at $64.5M in 2024, expected to reach $295.5M by 2034 with 16.4% CAGR. IoT integration in agriculture driving growth.
news.summary: A report on the Asia-Pacific Digital Soil Mapping Platforms and Applications Market has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The market was valued at $64.5 million in 2024 and is projected to reach $295.5 million by 2034, displaying a 16.4% CAGR from 2024 to 2034. The Asia-Pacific region is witnessing substantial growth in this market sector.

news.headline: Rapaport hosts Sixth Trade Mission to Sierra Leone, promoting ethical trade and economic development.

Sierra Leone, previously known for its civil war, is now a symbol of resilience and growth. This mission highlights the commitment to supporting the country's development.

The tour follows a successful performance in South Africa last year, attracting crowds in the thousands. news.headline: Elute receives FDA clearance for synthetic bone void filler kit, customizable granule sizes for patients.
news.summary: A cutting-edge kit system has been developed that allows for the customization of granule sizes based on the specific anatomy of patients in real time. This innovation is set to revolutionize the treatment of bone loss or defects resulting from trauma or infections for a large number of patients in the United States. Elute, Inc., a clinical organization based in Salt Lake City, unveiled this breakthrough on March 12, 2025. This development is expected to benefit thousands of patients by providing tailored treatment solutions to address their individual needs.

news.headline: Pusan National University study reveals nanoplastic toxicity in zebrafish, disrupting red blood cell maturation.
news.summary: Research conducted in Busan, South Korea on March 12, 2025, examines the effects of nanoplastic exposure on red blood cell maturation. It reveals that polystyrene nanoparticles, commonly found in consumer goods, can interfere with the development of red blood cells. This discovery underscores potential dangers to aquatic life. The study emphasizes the importance of recognizing the risks posed by nanoplastics and their impact on organisms.
